dataset: BBBC005 (Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection - Synthetic Cells)

pi_items.source: Direct download and file inspection; https://bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005
Unzip listing of bbbc005_test.zip: 9,600 w1 format images confirmed

pi_items.verified_value: Filename format is SIMCEPImages_WELL_CCELLS_FBLUR_sSAMPLES_wSTAIN.TIF where FBLUR ranges from 1-48

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated, but task description says F1=Sharpest, F5=Blurriest
pi_items.verified_value: Blur levels range from F1 to F48 (not F1-F5). F1 is in-focus (sharpest), F48 is most blurred.
pi_items.source: BBBC005 official documentation states 'The amount of focus blur applied (1-48)'. Verified with sample images: F1 VoL=15485.33, F4 VoL=14982.45, F48 VoL=9445.72

pi_items.metadata_value: Not stated in metadata, but implied by task context
pi_items.verified_value: Data is synthetic, generated using SIMCEP (Simulating Cell Population Images) platform with Gaussian blur filters
pi_items.source: https://bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005 states: 'Simulated HCS images were generated with the SIMCEP simulating platform' and 'Focus blur was simulated by applying Gaussian filters to the images.'
BBBC005 documentation and verified with sample images

pi_items.verified_value: Ground truth (segmentation masks) available only for F1 images (1,200 files total)
pi_items.source: https://bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005: 'The images with F1 in the filename are classified as fully in-focus for ground truth.'

pi_items.verified_value: Column list is consistent with task requirements for focus quality metrics and ranking
pi_items.source: Task description specifies calculation of these four focus metrics plus ranking and classification logic

pi_items.metadata_value: Not stated in metadata, but verifier.py line 136 uses r'_F(\d)_' regex
pi_items.verified_value: Verifier regex only matches single-digit F values (F1-F9), missing F10-F48. This means ~73% of images (those with multi-digit blur levels) would not be captured in correlation analysis.
pi_items.source: Code inspection of verifier.py and regex testing. Sample of 15 filenames: single-digit matches = 26.7%, multi-digit matches = 100%

privileged_info_summary: BBBC005 is a confirmed real dataset of 9,600 synthetic fluorescence microscopy images generated using the SIMCEP platform. Images have varying focus blur levels from F1 (sharp) to F48 (blurry), not F1-F5 as the task description suggests. Each image is 696×520 pixels in 8-bit TIF format. The data includes w1 images (cell body stain) with ground truth segmentation masks available only for the 1,200 F1 (in-focus) images. The verifier uses a regex pattern that only captures single-digit blur levels (F1-F9), potentially missing 73% of the dataset for correlation analysis.
